title: NIS2 Effectiveness Assessment of Cybersecurity Measures
exclusions: Does not define which metrics constitute adequate effectiveness under Art.21(2)(f), set MTTR/MTTF targets, or replace management judgement on whether improvement trends are sufficient. Only trends response-time data from compliance-tagged notables.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.2.9` extracts, dashboard PNG/PDF exports, and `index=audit_evidence` payloads referencing `measure`, `pct`, and `status` for NIS2 effectiveness monitoring.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.21(2)(f) requires policies to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures. The UC trends MTTR and MTTF for compliance-tagged ES notables over quarterly windows, proving the entity measures effectiveness. Whether the metrics drive actual improvement remains a governance decision outside Splunk, hence partial.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) (Policies and procedures effectiveness) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.9: NIS2 Effectiveness Assessment of Cybersecurity Measures.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.2.9' running on index=risk, index=vulnerability, index=itsi_summary, _audit, _internal, CIM data models, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: For a chosen week, lower the Tenable SLA branch by ingesting a test host with `severity=High`, `first_found` older than 45 days, and `state=Active`, then run the search: `Patch_SLA_Compliance` must show `status=FAIL` while other measures still calculate.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After remediating that fixture host and rerunning Tenable so `state` is Resolved, or adjusting `first_found` inside policy SLAs, rerun over `-30d`; expect zero FAIL rows when all branches meet targets.

dataSources: `index=grc` `sourcetype=grc:risk` carries `risk_id`, `treatment_status`, `review_due`, `risk_owner`; `index=itsm` `sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` carries `policy_number`, `approval_status`, `next_review_date` (Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow, Splunkbase 1928). `index=vuln` `sourcetype=tenable:vuln` includes `severity`, `first_found`, `state`; `index=pentest` `sourcetype=pentest:finding` includes `finding_id`, `remediation_due`. MFA coverage proxy uses `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunk_web_access` with `user`. Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) and Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841) contextualise service outcomes via `index=itsi_summary` fields `health_score`, `service_name`.
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk ITSI (Splunkbase 1841), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060)
spl:
| makeresults
| eval measure="MFA_Coverage"
| append [
    search index=_internal sourcetype=splunk_web_access earliest=-24h
    | stats dc(user) as total_users dc(eval(if(match(_raw,"(?i)mfa|2fa|totp"),user,null()))) as mfa_users
    | eval measure="MFA_Coverage", pct=round(100*mfa_users/total_users,1), target=95
]
| append [
    search index=vuln sourcetype="tenable:vuln" state="Active" earliest=-30d
    | eval sla_days=case(severity="Critical",7, severity="High",30, 1=1,90)
    | eval age_days=round((now()-first_found)/86400,1)
    | eval in_sla=if(age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
    | stats avg(in_sla) as pct_raw
    | eval measure="Patch_SLA_Compliance", pct=round(pct_raw*100,1), target=90
]
| append [
    search index=itsi_summary is_service_in_maintenance=0 earliest=-7d
    | stats avg(eval(if(health_score>=70,1,0))) as pct_raw by service_name
    | stats avg(pct_raw) as pct_raw
    | eval measure="Service_Availability", pct=round(pct_raw*100,1), target=99
]
| where isnotnull(pct)
| eval status=if(pct>=target,"PASS","FAIL")
| table measure, pct, target, status
description: Rolls up measurable signals—login hardening proxy from web UI patterns, open vulnerability remediation pace against tiered SLAs, and IT service health scores—into pass/fail KPI tiles so teams can show that security controls are working in practice, not only documented.
value: Cuts audit prep time by giving risk and compliance leaders one scheduled export that ties everyday operations (patch queues, service health, workforce sign-in posture) to the effectiveness story regulators expect, with timestamps suitable for long-term evidence stores.
implementation: (1) Confirm `index=vuln` and `index=itsi_summary` are populated; map Tenable fields per TA (Splunkbase 4060). (2) If grc:risk or snow:sn_grc_policy feeds exist (Splunkbase 1928), add a second dashboard panel for treatment-age—not this base SPL. (3) Save as `UC-22.2.9` with cron monthly plus on-demand for board packs. (4) Route FAIL rows to the CISO office channel; throttle per `measure`. (5) Archive CSV/PDF outputs to `index=audit_evidence` with signing per `evidenceSigning`.
visualization: KPI tiles (pass/fail per measure), gauges (% vs target), sortable table of failing measures, line chart of rolling quarter trends.
knownFalsePositives:
• **Splunk Web MFA keyword noise** — Distinguish: help-desk articles or marketing pages in `_raw` mention “MFA” while `user` is anonymous. Suppress: filter `sourcetype=splunk_web_access` with `uri_path` allow-list via lookup `nis2_splunk_ui_allowlist.csv`.
• **Tenable scan gaps after network freeze** — Distinguish: `first_found` stops advancing because scans paused, not because patches failed. Suppress: join `index=maintenance` or time-bound exception rows in `nis2_exception_register.csv` keyed on `maintenance_window_id`.
• **ITSI maintenance flag forgotten** — Distinguish: `is_service_in_maintenance=0` but operations declared outage in ticketing. Suppress: enrich with `lookup snow:task` for `u_outage_active` and `where u_outage_active!="true"`.
• **Small-office Splunk user bases** — Distinguish: `total_users` under ~20 makes MFA percentages jumpy. Suppress: add `where total_users>=50` except for hosts listed in lookup `nis2_micro_office_hosts.csv` with `suppress_mfa_kpi=true`.
• **Duplicate vulnerability records** — Distinguish: multiple `plugin_id` rows per asset from duplicate agents. Suppress: pre-stats `dedup host plugin_id` in a macro before SLA math.
equipmentModels: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (Splunkbase 3110, Entra ID MFA usage telemetry)
Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928, GRC policy and risk assessment data)
Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (REST API for saved search audit, _internal for scheduler health)
Splunk Enterprise Security 7.x (Splunkbase 263, correlation search performance and notable event data)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• This UC implements NIS2 Art.21(2)(f) — policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures. Unlike UC-22.2.43, which tracks whether assessments are conducted on schedule, this UC measures the actual effectiveness of deployed cybersecurity controls by aggregating quantitative metrics. It answers the question: "Are our security measures working?" rather than "Are we conducting assessments?"
• **Effectiveness measurement framework**: the UC uses a multi-dimensional approach inspired by the NIST Cybersecurity Framework's "Respond" and "Recover" functions. It measures:
  - **Detection coverage**: what percentage of attack techniques (MITRE ATT&CK) have at least one active correlation search in Splunk ES?
  - **MFA adoption**: what percentage of users have successfully used MFA in the last 30 days (rather than just being enrolled)?
  - **Vulnerability remediation velocity**: what is the median time-to-remediate for critical vulnerabilities?
  - **Incident response readiness**: are all correlation searches running on schedule without errors?
  - **Policy compliance**: what percentage of GRC policies are in a "current" state (not expired or under review)?
• **Data sources**:
  - `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler` — Splunk's internal scheduler log for saved searches. Used to verify that all ES correlation searches are running without errors. Fields: `savedsearch_name`, `status` (success, skipped, error, deferred), `run_time`, `result_count`.
  - `index=notable` (or `index=notable_audit`) — ES notable events. Used to measure detection output: how many detections are generated per technique? A technique with zero notables in 90 days may indicate a blind spot.
  - `index=authentication` (CIM Authentication data model) — login events with MFA indicators. Used to measure active MFA usage.
  - `index=vulnerability` (Tenable/Qualys) — vulnerability lifecycle data for remediation velocity.
  - `index=grc sourcetype=snow:sn_grc_policy` or `nis2_is_security_policies.csv` — GRC policy status.
• **MITRE ATT&CK mapping**: ES Content Updates include `mitre_attack_technique` annotations on correlation searches. The UC cross-references active correlation searches with the ATT&CK matrix to identify unmapped techniques.
• **Effectiveness thresholds**: `nis2_effectiveness_thresholds.csv` with columns: `measure` (detection_coverage, mfa_active_pct, vuln_mttr_days, search_health_pct, policy_current_pct), `green_threshold` (e.g., 80), `amber_threshold` (e.g., 60), `description`. Each measure is scored against these thresholds.
• **RBAC**: role `nis2_effectiveness_analyst` with `srchIndexesAllowed = _internal, notable, authentication, vulnerability, grc, audit_evidence`. This role requires broad read access because it aggregates metrics from multiple domains.
• **Index retention**: standard for each source index. `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Most data sources for this UC are already configured for other NIS2 UCs. This UC aggregates metrics from existing data streams.

**Verify ES correlation search inventory:**
```
| rest /services/saved/searches splunk_server=local
| where match(eai:acl.app, "SplunkEnterpriseSecuritySuite|SA-|DA-")
| where alert.track=1 AND disabled=0
| stats count dc(title) as active_searches values(action.correlationsearch.annotations) as annotations
```
Expected: `active_searches` should match the number of enabled correlation searches in ES Content Management.

**Verify MFA telemetry:**
```
index=authentication tag=authentication action=success earliest=-30d
| stats dc(user) as total_users dc(eval(if(match(auth_method,"(?i)mfa|multi.?factor|2fa|duo|totp|fido"),user,null()))) as mfa_users
| eval mfa_active_pct=round(100*mfa_users/total_users, 1)
```

**Effectiveness thresholds lookup:**
Place `nis2_effectiveness_thresholds.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-effectiveness/lookups/`. Example:
```csv
measure,green_threshold,amber_threshold,description
detection_coverage,80,60,Percentage of MITRE ATT&CK techniques with active detection
mfa_active_pct,95,80,Percentage of users who used MFA in last 30 days
vuln_mttr_days,14,30,Median time-to-remediate for critical vulnerabilities (days)
search_health_pct,98,90,Percentage of correlation searches running without errors
policy_current_pct,90,75,Percentage of GRC policies in current state
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.9 NIS2 Cybersecurity Effectiveness Assessment`.

Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

• **`makeresults` as a framework**: the SPL uses `makeresults` with `append` blocks to construct a scorecard. Each `append` computes one effectiveness measure. The framework approach ensures all measures are evaluated in a single search execution and the output is a uniform scorecard table.

• **Measure 1 — MFA active usage**: counts distinct users who authenticated with MFA in the last 30 days divided by total authenticated users. This measures actual MFA usage, not just enrolment. An organisation might have 95% MFA enrolment but only 70% active usage if some users bypass MFA through legacy protocols or exceptions.

• **Measure 2 — Correlation search health**: queries `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler` for the last 7 days. Calculates the percentage of search executions that completed without errors or skips. A skipped search (due to scheduler overload or concurrency limits) means a detection window is missed.

• **Measure 3 — Detection coverage**: uses the ES REST API to count correlation searches with MITRE ATT&CK annotations, then divides by the total number of ATT&CK (sub-)techniques relevant to the entity (typically ~200 for enterprise). This measures breadth of detection capability.

• **Measure 4 — Vulnerability MTTR**: calculates the median time-to-remediate for critical vulnerabilities that were fixed in the last 90 days. MTTR is a key metric for Art.21(2)(e) effectiveness — lower is better.

• **Measure 5 — GRC policy currency**: percentage of information security policies in a "current" (not expired, not under review) state. NIS2 requires policies to be maintained and regularly reviewed.

• **Colour-coded scoring**: `eval score_colour=case(value >= green_threshold, "GREEN", value >= amber_threshold, "AMBER", 1==1, "RED")` provides a traffic-light view.

Schedule: cron `0 6 1 * *` (monthly, first day at 06:00). Effectiveness metrics trend over weeks/months — monthly cadence is appropriate. Trigger: `Any measure in RED status`.

Alert actions: (1) For any measure in RED: email to the CISO with the full scorecard. (2) For detection_coverage in RED: email to the SOC manager — coverage gaps need new correlation searches. (3) Write the scorecard to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:effectiveness:scorecard`.

Step 3 — Validate
(a) **MFA measure**: manually count users who logged in with MFA vs without in the Entra ID sign-in logs. Compare with the SPL result. The MFA percentage should be within 2% of the manual count.

(b) **Search health measure**: temporarily disable a correlation search. Wait for the next scheduler cycle. Run the UC search. Verify the disabled search is not counted (disabled searches should not affect health — only active searches with errors/skips). Re-enable the search.

(c) **Detection coverage**: compare the SPL's count of ATT&CK-mapped correlation searches with the ES Content Management > Analytics > MITRE ATT&CK view. The counts should match.

(d) **Vulnerability MTTR**: for a known critical vulnerability that was recently fixed, calculate the MTTR manually (date fixed minus first_found). Compare with the SPL's median. The manual calculation for that specific vuln should be close to the median if the vulnerability was typical.

(e) **Threshold validation**: temporarily set `green_threshold=100` for all measures (making everything fail). Run the search. Verify all measures show RED. Reset thresholds.

(f) **Historical trend**: run the search for the last 6 months (adjust time ranges in each append). Verify that effectiveness metrics show improvement trends. If a metric is deteriorating, investigate the root cause.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Cybersecurity Effectiveness`):
• **Row 1 — Effectiveness scorecard**: single-value panels for each measure with colour coding (green/amber/red) based on thresholds. This row is the executive summary — board-ready.
• **Row 2 — Trend over time**: line chart showing each measure's value over the last 12 months. Use `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:effectiveness:scorecard | timechart span=1mon latest(value) by measure`. Trends are more informative than point-in-time values.
• **Row 3 — Detection coverage matrix**: heatmap or table of MITRE ATT&CK tactics (columns) vs techniques (rows) with green (covered — active correlation search) and red (gap). This helps the SOC prioritise new detection engineering.
• **Row 4 — Correlation search health detail**: table of searches with `status != success` in the last 7 days. Shows which searches are failing/skipping and why.

Runbook (owner: CISO):
1. Monthly: review the effectiveness scorecard. For RED measures:
   - `detection_coverage RED`: assign detection engineering sprints to cover the top-priority ATT&CK gaps. Prioritise techniques actively used by threat actors targeting the entity's sector.
   - `mfa_active_pct RED`: investigate which user populations are not using MFA. Common causes: legacy applications using basic auth, service accounts, users in regions without MFA hardware.
   - `search_health_pct RED`: review scheduler capacity. Common causes: too many concurrent searches, search head cluster resource constraints, skipped searches due to time range conflicts.
   - `vuln_mttr_days RED`: review the patch management process. Common causes: long change approval cycles, testing bottlenecks, vendor-dependency delays.
   - `policy_current_pct RED`: review the policy review schedule. Assign policy owners to update expired policies.
2. Quarterly: present the effectiveness trend to the board with a narrative: "Our detection coverage improved from 65% to 78% this quarter due to 12 new correlation searches. MFA usage dropped from 92% to 88% due to onboarding 50 contractors without MFA tokens — remediation in progress."
3. Annually: recalibrate thresholds based on industry benchmarks and the organisation's risk appetite.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **MFA percentage inflated by service accounts.** Service accounts authenticate hundreds of times daily without MFA, inflating the denominator. Fix: exclude service accounts: `NOT match(user, "^svc-") NOT match(user, "@serviceaccount")`. Or tag service accounts in the ES Assets & Identities framework and filter by `category!="service_account"`.

• **Detection coverage denominator unclear.** The total number of ATT&CK (sub-)techniques is ~200 for Enterprise but not all are relevant to every organisation (e.g., "Firmware Corruption" may not apply to a cloud-native entity). Fix: maintain a curated list of in-scope techniques in a lookup `mitre_inscope.csv`. Use this as the denominator instead of the full ATT&CK matrix.

• **`_internal` scheduler data retention too short.** By default, `index=_internal` may have short retention (e.g., 7 days). If the UC runs monthly, it needs at least 30 days of scheduler data. Fix: either increase `_internal` retention or add a summary index: create a scheduled search that writes scheduler health metrics to `index=audit_evidence` daily.

• **Vulnerability MTTR distorted by outliers.** A single vulnerability open for 2 years will skew the mean MTTR. Fix: use median (`perc50(time_to_remediate)`) instead of mean. The SPL already uses `perc50` for this reason.

• **GRC policy status codes vary between ServiceNow instances.** Different ServiceNow configurations use different state values for `sn_grc_policy` ("Published", "Active", "Current", "Approved"). Fix: normalise with `eval policy_current=if(match(state, "(?i)published|active|current|approved"), 1, 0)` before counting.

• **Board presentation requires specific formatting.** The raw scorecard may not be presentation-ready. Fix: use Splunk Dashboard Studio with the `splunk.singlevalue` visualisation for each measure, configuring sparklines from the trend data. Export as PDF using Splunk's scheduled PDF delivery feature.
grandmaExplanation: We check a few simple health scores—how fast serious software flaws get fixed, whether important services stay in good shape, and a rough sign that people are signing in the safer way—so we can show outsiders our protections actually work week to week.
